isaacBeginnerGuide.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Beginner Guide
isaacBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the room-clearing loop, protect your health, spend pickups wisely, and follow a proven path from Basement to Mom and Mom's Heart in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.
isaacBeginnerGuide.intro: Each run in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance is a chain of randomly generated floors. Clear combat rooms, collect resources, improve your build through special rooms, defeat the floor boss, and descend. Early success depends more on avoiding damage and making consistent decisions than on finding one perfect item.

steps.title: Learn Movement, Shooting, and Room Control
steps.goal: Move and fire independently while preserving a safe escape path.
details: Movement and firing use separate directional controls. Bombs, active items, cards, pills, and trinket drops have their own inputs, which can be remapped in the options menu.
Most enemies aim at Isaac's current position. Use short, deliberate movements instead of circling the room without watching projectiles.
Destroy chargers, projectile-heavy enemies, and enemy spawners before slow melee enemies.
checklist: Practice moving in one direction while firing in another.
Keep at least one open lane for dodging.
Do not stand against a wall unless the attack pattern requires it.

steps.title: Understand Health and Core Stats
steps.goal: Recognize whether an item improves offense, survival, or consistency.
details: Red Heart containers can be refilled and are commonly used to pay for Devil Deals. Soul and Black Hearts protect health and help preserve deal-room chances.

Damage controls how hard tears hit. Fire Rate controls shooting frequency. Range controls travel distance, Shot Speed controls projectile speed, Speed controls movement, and Luck affects many chance-based effects.
Enable Found HUD in the options menu to display numerical stats during a run.
checklist: Prioritize an early damage or fire-rate upgrade.
Keep enough movement speed to dodge comfortably.
Treat Soul and Black Hearts as both health and deal protection.

steps.title: Clear Rooms in the Right Order
steps.goal: Remove immediate threats before chasing pickups or breaking objects.
details: Most combat rooms remain locked until all required enemies are defeated. Clearing a room may create a pickup and recharge active items.
Use rocks and pits as cover against grounded enemies, but remember that flying enemies and spectral projectiles can ignore some obstacles.
Save bombs during easy rooms unless using one prevents more damage than the bomb is worth.
checklist: Identify the fastest or most dangerous enemy immediately.
Move before firing when a room begins crowded.
Finish slow enemies only after the room is under control.

steps.title: Spend Keys, Bombs, and Coins by Priority
steps.goal: Turn limited resources into dependable upgrades.
details: Treasure Rooms contain a free pedestal item. Most Treasure Room doors after the first floor require one key.
Shops require a key and sell items, health, batteries, cards, and other pickups. Donating spare coins improves future shops.
Bombs reveal hidden rooms, open tinted rocks, damage bosses, and destroy obstacles. Keys also open locked chests and special doors.
checklist: Reserve an early key for the Treasure Room unless another door has clearly higher value.
Look for tinted rocks with a faint cross and different color.
Keep a bomb for a strong Secret Room location or a dangerous boss.

steps.title: Recognize Valuable Special Rooms
steps.goal: Know what a room offers before paying its entry cost.
details: Treasure Rooms are the main source of build-changing items. Shops provide controlled purchases and utility.
Libraries contain books, Arcades trade money or health for rewards, and Curse Rooms may contain Red Chests, Devil items, pickups, or enemies.
Sacrifice Rooms provide increasingly powerful rewards after repeated spike damage, but beginners should use them only with spare healing.
checklist: Visit the Treasure Room on most early floors.
Check the Shop when you have a key and at least 15 coins.
Do not enter a paid-health room when the cost could prevent you from surviving the boss.

steps.title: Choose Between Devil Deals and Angel Rooms
steps.goal: Take premium items without destroying your survivability.
details: Devil Rooms can appear after bosses from the second floor onward and usually sell powerful items for health.
Taking Red Heart damage reduces the current floor's deal chance, especially during the boss fight. Soul and Black Heart damage does not apply the same penalty.

In Repentance, refusing to enter the first available Devil Room guarantees that the next successful deal-room spawn will be an Angel Room. Entering the room but refusing its items keeps a mixed Devil and Angel route.
checklist: Take a Devil Deal only when the reward clearly outweighs the lost health.
Skip the first Devil Room completely when targeting free Angel items.
After Angel battles are unlocked, bomb two Angel statues to assemble the key for Mega Satan.

steps.title: Find Secret Rooms Efficiently
steps.goal: Use floor geometry to make high-value bomb checks.
details: A regular Secret Room usually touches several ordinary rooms. Each connecting wall needs a valid doorway position without rocks, pits, or obstacles blocking it.
A Super Secret Room normally connects to only one room and is frequently found near the later portion of the floor or the route toward the boss.
Place the bomb at the center of the suspected wall. Test strong three-room connections before uncertain two-room locations.
checklist: Check whether every adjacent wall has a clear doorway position.
Test locations touching three normal rooms first.
Stop searching when the remaining bombs are more valuable for tinted rocks or combat.

steps.title: Follow the First-Playthrough Route
steps.goal: Reach Mom with a stable build instead of rushing.
details: Start as Isaac on Normal Mode and progress through Basement I-II, Caves I-II, and Depths I-II.
On each floor, find the Treasure Room, consider the Shop, then defeat the boss. Full-clear only when you still need health, keys, bombs, coins, charges, or a special room.
Mom is the fixed boss of Depths II on the first route. Keep moving away from the stomping foot, watch all four doors for attacks, and use bombs when she is exposed.
checklist: Find at least one meaningful offensive upgrade before Depths.
Enter Mom's room with spare health and one or two bombs.
Defeating Mom unlocks the Womb and the first ending.

steps.title: Defeat Mom's Heart and Open the Endgame
steps.goal: Turn early victories into permanent routes and characters.
details: After defeating Mom once, later runs continue through Womb I-II. Most damage in the Womb and later chapters removes a full heart.
Mom's Heart becomes protected while summoned enemies remain alive. Clear the summons quickly, then damage the Heart while moving carefully through bullet patterns.
The tenth Mom's Heart defeat unlocks ???. The eleventh defeat changes the boss into It Lives!, providing regular access to Cathedral and Sheol.
checklist: Save defensive active items for dense projectile patterns.
Destroy summoned enemies before focusing on the Heart.
After unlocking It Lives!, work toward five Isaac wins for The Polaroid and five Satan wins for The Negative.

quickTips: Enable Found HUD early so you can read your damage, tears, range, and luck at a glance.
Keep a bomb for a strong Secret Room location or a dangerous boss instead of spending it freely.
Skip the first Devil Room to guarantee an Angel Room when you want free items and Mega Satan access.
Look for tinted rocks with a faint cross for guaranteed pickups, hearts, or items.

isaacItemTierList.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Item Tier List
isaacItemTierList.subtitle: Compare high-impact items and consumables by consistency, item pool, unlock requirement, synergy value, and potential downside in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.
isaacItemTierList.intro: This tier list favors items that perform reliably across many characters and builds. Lower-tier entries are not always weak: several become exceptional with the correct synergy, but they require more planning or create greater risk than universally strong upgrades.

tiers.label: Run-Defining
tiers.description: Items that can transform an average run into a winning one with little additional setup.

entries.name: Sacred Heart
entries.effect: Provides a massive damage multiplier, homing tears, one Red Heart container, healing, and additional tear changes.
entries.best_use: Take on nearly any conventional tear build; homing makes slow or easily missed projectiles far more consistent.
entries.caution: It lowers fire rate, but the damage and homing usually outweigh the penalty.
entries.unlock: Available in the Angel Room pool.

entries.name: C Section
entries.effect: Replaces tears with homing fetus projectiles that repeatedly damage enemies and inherit many tear effects.
entries.best_use: Combine with damage, multishot, homing support, and compatible tear effects.
entries.caution: Some tear replacements override or significantly change its behavior.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Lilith.

entries.name: Glitched Crown
entries.effect: Most item pedestals rapidly cycle through several possible items, creating repeated chances to choose a premium result.
entries.best_use: Watch the full cycle and time the pickup deliberately.
entries.caution: Poor timing can select the wrong item, while blind pedestals remove much of its control.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Isaac.

entries.name: Spindown Dice
entries.effect: Rerolls a pedestal into the item with the previous internal item ID, allowing predictable transformations.
entries.best_use: Save charges for known conversions into premium items and combine it with batteries or charge generation.
entries.caution: Its strongest use requires learning conversion chains.
entries.unlock: Defeat Delirium as Tainted Isaac.

entries.name: R Key
entries.effect: Restarts the run at Basement I while preserving collected items and achievement eligibility.
entries.best_use: Complete one late-game target, then restart with the existing build to pursue another route or collect more completion marks.
entries.caution: The timer does not reset, so missed Boss Rush and Hush limits remain missed.
entries.unlock: No character completion unlock is required.

entries.name: Twisted Pair
entries.effect: Adds two familiars that copy the player's firing direction and scale with player damage.
entries.best_use: Provides immediate damage and benefits from many global tear effects.
entries.caution: The separated firing positions can miss small enemies at unusual angles.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Lilith.

entries.name: Sacred Orb
entries.effect: Filters many weak item results and rerolls some lower-quality pedestals into better options.
entries.best_use: Take it early so later Treasure, Boss, Angel, Devil, and Secret Room rewards benefit.
entries.caution: It improves future item quality rather than providing immediate combat power.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Lost.

tiers.label: Excellent
tiers.description: Powerful options that consistently improve most builds but fall slightly below the most dominant run-defining items.

entries.name: Brimstone
entries.effect: Replaces tears with a charged, piercing blood laser.
entries.best_use: Excellent against groups and bosses, especially with homing, split-shot, or multishot effects.
entries.caution: Charge time changes the firing rhythm and some tear effects behave differently.
entries.unlock: Available in the Devil Room pool.

entries.name: Mom's Knife
entries.effect: Replaces tears with a chargeable knife that deals very high repeated contact damage.
entries.best_use: Release the knife through bosses and use its return path for additional hits.
entries.caution: It overrides many tear combinations and often requires closer positioning.
entries.unlock: Defeat Satan as Isaac.

entries.name: Tech X
entries.effect: Fires chargeable laser rings that pierce enemies and cover large areas.
entries.best_use: Use medium or large rings to control rooms; damage, homing, and multishot scale it well.
entries.caution: Very small rings can underperform when released too quickly.
entries.unlock: Available in the Treasure Room pool.

entries.name: Godhead
entries.effect: Adds homing, a damaging aura around tears, and a major damage increase.
entries.best_use: Especially powerful with slow, large, piercing, or lingering projectiles.
entries.caution: Obtaining the unlock requires completing The Lost's Hard Mode marks.
entries.unlock: Earn every required Hard Mode completion mark as The Lost.

entries.name: D Infinity
entries.effect: Cycles between several dice effects for rerolling items, pickups, stats, or the full build.
entries.best_use: Select the die that solves the current room instead of carrying separate reroll tools.
entries.caution: The wrong die can destroy a strong build or valuable room.
entries.unlock: Defeat Delirium as Isaac.

entries.name: The Stairway
entries.effect: Creates a ladder in the starting room of later floors leading to a shop that sells Angel Room items.
entries.best_use: Maintain a coin reserve and buy repeated Angel items over multiple floors.
entries.caution: The ladder disappears after leaving the starting room.
entries.unlock: Defeat Isaac as Jacob and Esau.

entries.name: Birthright
entries.effect: Provides a unique character-specific improvement.
entries.best_use: Its effects can add item slots, health, familiars, damage, or major mechanic upgrades depending on the character.
entries.caution: Value varies greatly between characters.
entries.unlock: Defeat ??? as Jacob and Esau.

entries.name: Incubus
entries.effect: Copies the player's tears and most tear effects from the familiar's position.
entries.best_use: Acts as a large damage multiplier for conventional tear builds.
entries.caution: Offset positioning can make shots miss in narrow rooms.
entries.unlock: Defeat Hush as Lilith.

entries.name: Curved Horn
entries.effect: Provides a flat damage increase while held.
entries.best_use: A simple early-game power increase with no health or charge cost.
entries.caution: It competes with defensive and utility trinkets.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Lamb as Judas.

entries.name: Jera
entries.effect: Duplicates pickups and unopened chests in the current room.
entries.best_use: Save it for battery rooms, chest-heavy endgame floors, or rooms containing several valuable pickups.
entries.caution: It does not directly duplicate pedestal items.
entries.unlock: Complete Challenge #2, High Brow.

entries.name: Chaos Card
entries.effect: Throws a card that instantly defeats many enemies and bosses it touches.
entries.best_use: Reserve it for a difficult boss or a room that threatens the run.
entries.caution: The card must be aimed, can miss, and does not bypass every final-boss phase.
entries.unlock: Complete Challenge #9, Demo Man.

tiers.label: Dependable and Flexible
tiers.description: Strong tools that improve many runs but usually require charges, health management, positioning, or a compatible route.

entries.name: Satanic Bible
entries.effect: Grants a Black Heart and changes the current floor's boss reward into a purchasable Devil item.
entries.best_use: Generate defense while replacing weak boss rewards with potentially stronger Devil items.
entries.caution: Repeated purchases can consume too many heart containers.
entries.unlock: Defeat Isaac as Azazel.

entries.name: Book of Virtues
entries.effect: Creates orbiting wisps and combines with most active items to create unique wisp effects.
entries.best_use: Pair with a low-charge active item to build offense and projectile protection.
entries.caution: Wisps can be destroyed by enemy shots and contact.
entries.unlock: Defeat Isaac as Bethany.

entries.name: The Pact
entries.effect: Provides damage, fire rate, and Soul Hearts.
entries.best_use: A dependable all-purpose offensive and defensive upgrade.
entries.caution: The Devil Deal price may still be unsafe on low-health characters.
entries.unlock: Available in the Devil Room pool.

entries.name: Rotten Baby
entries.effect: Spawns blue flies while the player fires.
entries.best_use: Scales with player damage and adds automatic burst damage.
entries.caution: Flies may be wasted on weak targets before reaching a boss.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Lamb as Lilith.

entries.name: Holy Mantle
entries.effect: Blocks the first hit of damage in each room.
entries.best_use: Excellent for Curse Rooms, difficult rooms, and characters with one-hit health mechanics.
entries.caution: After the shield breaks, it provides no further protection until the next room.
entries.unlock: Available as an item; The Lost starts with it after 879 Greed Donation Machine coins.

entries.name: Diplopia
entries.effect: Duplicates pedestal items and pickups in the current room.
entries.best_use: Copy an Angel item, Devil selection, shop inventory, or room with several valuable pickups.
entries.caution: Using it early can waste a stronger later duplication opportunity.
entries.unlock: Available in the Shop pool.

entries.name: Credit Card
entries.effect: Makes purchases in the current Shop or Devil Room free.
entries.best_use: Use in a room containing several expensive items.
entries.caution: Holding it for too long risks never finding a premium room.
entries.unlock: Complete Challenge #10, Cursed!.

entries.name: Perthro
entries.effect: Rerolls pedestal items in the current room.
entries.best_use: Replace weak Treasure, Boss, Angel, or Devil items.
entries.caution: The original item cannot be recovered after rerolling.
entries.unlock: Complete Challenge #6, Solar System.

entries.name: Soul of Isaac
entries.effect: Makes pedestals alternate between the original item and a rerolled option.
entries.best_use: Gain a second choice without immediately losing the original result.
entries.caution: Stepping onto the pedestal at the wrong moment selects the wrong item.
entries.unlock: Complete Boss Rush and defeat Hush as Tainted Isaac.

entries.name: Cracked Crown
entries.effect: Improves several stats that are already above their base values.
entries.best_use: Strong on balanced builds containing several small stat upgrades.
entries.caution: It provides less value when few base stats have been improved.
entries.unlock: Win five Daily Challenges consecutively.

tiers.label: Build-Dependent
tiers.description: These items can become excellent, but their drawbacks or control changes can damage an incompatible build.

entries.name: Soy Milk
entries.effect: Greatly increases fire rate while applying a severe damage multiplier.
entries.best_use: Combine with flat damage additions, homing, and effects that trigger per tear.
entries.caution: Weak individual hits can create excessive knockback and poor enemy control.

entries.name: Almond Milk
entries.effect: Greatly increases fire rate, lowers damage, and applies changing worm-style tear patterns.
entries.best_use: Works with powerful effects that benefit from producing many projectiles.
entries.caution: Random trajectories reduce accuracy.

entries.name: Tiny Planet
entries.effect: Makes tears orbit around the player with spectral behavior and increased range.
entries.best_use: Combine with piercing, lasers, homing, or large projectiles.
entries.caution: Normal tears may miss nearby targets and require a different aiming style.

entries.name: Ludovico Technique
entries.effect: Creates one controllable tear that moves independently from the player.
entries.best_use: Very strong with Brimstone, Technology effects, homing, or large damaging auras.
entries.caution: A weak or slow tear can leave the player unable to answer threats from several directions.

entries.name: Ipecac
entries.effect: Fires arcing explosive tears with a very large damage increase.
entries.best_use: Clears rooms and bosses quickly while providing unlimited rock destruction.
entries.caution: Close-range enemies and unusual tear paths can cause self-damage.

entries.name: Dr. Fetus
entries.effect: Replaces tears with fired bombs that inherit many bomb effects.
entries.best_use: Powerful with bomb upgrades, homing, and explosion immunity.
entries.caution: Explosion timing and knockback can cause self-damage or slow clears.

entries.name: Cursed Eye
entries.effect: Charges and releases a burst of tears.
entries.best_use: Provides strong burst damage when fully charged from a safe position.
entries.caution: Taking damage while charging can teleport the player out of the room and reset boss progress.

entries.name: Bob's Brain
entries.effect: Launches an explosive familiar when firing toward an enemy.
entries.best_use: Offers strong early damage when spacing is controlled.
entries.caution: The explosion can damage the player, especially at short range.

entries.name: Magic Skin
entries.effect: Creates an item from the current room's pool while adding a Broken Heart.
entries.best_use: Use sparingly in premium pools such as Angel or Secret Rooms.
entries.caution: Repeated use reduces maximum health, and abandoned Magic Skin can replace later pedestals.

entries.name: Missing No.
entries.effect: Rerolls passive items at the start of each floor.
entries.best_use: Can rescue a losing build or create unusual high-roll combinations.
entries.caution: A winning build can be destroyed on the next floor without player control.

tiers.label: Reroll or Handle Carefully
tiers.description: Items with severe aiming, positioning, self-damage, or randomness risks unless a specific synergy removes the drawback.

entries.name: The Wiz
entries.effect: Adds double shots fired diagonally.
entries.best_use: Can work with homing, Tractor Beam, or wide laser effects.
entries.caution: Directly hitting enemies becomes difficult without a corrective synergy.

entries.name: Strange Attractor
entries.effect: Tears create a magnetic force that pulls enemies and objects toward their path.
entries.best_use: Can group enemies for piercing and area damage.
entries.caution: Dangerous enemies may be pulled directly into the player.

entries.name: Guillotine
entries.effect: Detaches Isaac's head as an orbital while increasing damage and fire rate.
entries.best_use: The orbital blocks projectiles and deals contact damage.
entries.caution: The visible head no longer represents the player's body hitbox, making dodging harder.

entries.name: Isaac's Heart
entries.effect: Makes the player's body invulnerable while a following heart becomes the damageable hitbox.
entries.best_use: Supports aggressive contact strategies and selected self-damage interactions.
entries.caution: The trailing heart is difficult to position and can be hit by attacks the body avoids.

entries.name: Curse of the Tower
entries.effect: Spawns multiple troll bombs whenever the player takes damage.
entries.best_use: Can deal heavy room damage with explosion immunity and bomb upgrades.
entries.caution: Without immunity, one hit may cause additional damage or destroy useful pickups.

entries.name: TMTRAINER
entries.effect: Replaces future items with randomized glitched effects.
entries.best_use: Designed for chaotic runs and can create extremely powerful combinations.
entries.caution: Effects may be useless, disruptive, or dangerous, making normal item planning impossible.
entries.unlock: Defeat The Beast as Tainted Eden.

isaacCharacters.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Characters and Tainted Characters
isaacCharacters.subtitle: Compare all 17 normal characters and 17 Tainted variants in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance by unlock method, starting tools, core mechanic, strengths, weaknesses, and difficulty.
isaacCharacters.intro: These tiers measure general consistency and accessibility rather than the quality of each character's unlock rewards. Permanent upgrades such as Isaac's D6, The Lost's Holy Mantle, and Keeper's Wooden Nickel can substantially improve a character after they are obtained.

tiers.label: Beginner-Friendly Powerhouses
tiers.description: Characters with strong starting tools, forgiving health, or mechanics that consistently create winning runs.

entries.name: Isaac
entries.unlock: Available from the start.
entries.starts_with: Three Red Hearts and one bomb; The D6 becomes his starting active after defeating Isaac as ???.
entries.core_mechanic: A balanced character whose D6 rerolls pedestal items.
strengths: Excellent item control with The D6
weaknesses: Base Isaac has no special offensive advantage
D6 value depends on charge management

entries.name: Magdalene
entries.unlock: Have seven or more Red Heart containers at one time.
entries.starts_with: Four Red Hearts and Yum Heart; completing Aprils Fool adds a Full Health pill.
entries.core_mechanic: Large starting health and repeatable healing make mistakes easier to recover from.
weaknesses: Healing cannot fix weak late-game damage

entries.name: Cain
entries.unlock: Hold 55 coins at one time.
entries.starts_with: Two Red Hearts, Lucky Foot, one key, and a pill; Paper Clip is added after 68 Greed Donation Machine coins.
entries.core_mechanic: Strong damage and speed combine with favorable pill and gambling outcomes.
weaknesses: Reduced firing angle and effective range

entries.name: Azazel
entries.unlock: Take three Devil Deal items in one run.
entries.starts_with: Three Black Hearts, flight, and a short-range Brimstone beam.
entries.core_mechanic: Extremely high early damage and permanent flight at the cost of short range.

entries.name: Tainted Judas
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Judas.
entries.starts_with: Two Black Hearts, three coins, and Dark Arts in the pocket active slot.
entries.core_mechanic: Dark Arts makes Judas intangible and converts enemies and shots passed through into a powerful ending slash.
strengths: Offense and defense in one ability
weaknesses: Poor timing can end inside a threat

entries.name: Tainted Lilith
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Lilith.
entries.starts_with: One Red Heart, two Black Hearts, and an attached fetus familiar used as her main attack.
entries.core_mechanic: Firing launches the fetus like a whip, dealing contact damage before it fires tears.
weaknesses: Narrow rooms can restrict the attack

tiers.label: Strong and Consistent
tiers.description: Powerful characters that remain dependable once their central mechanic is understood.

entries.name: Judas
entries.unlock: Defeat Satan.
entries.starts_with: One Red Heart, three coins, and Book of Belial.
entries.core_mechanic: High base damage combined with a repeatable boss-room damage boost.

entries.name: Eve
entries.unlock: Complete two consecutive floors without picking up hearts.
entries.starts_with: Two Red Hearts, Dead Bird, and Whore of Babylon; Razor Blade is added after 439 Greed Donation Machine coins.
entries.core_mechanic: Whore of Babylon grants major offensive bonuses while Eve remains at one Red Heart or lower.
weaknesses: Weak damage outside Whore of Babylon
Maintaining the bonus limits Red Heart protection

entries.name: Lilith
entries.unlock: Defeat Ultra Greed as Azazel.
entries.starts_with: One Red Heart, two Black Hearts, Incubus, Box of Friends, and Cambion Conception.
entries.core_mechanic: Lilith fires through Incubus, while Box of Friends temporarily duplicates her familiars.
strengths: Cambion Conception adds familiars over time
weaknesses: Her starting active competes with other actives

entries.name: Bethany
entries.unlock: Defeat Mom's Heart or It Lives! on Hard Mode as Lazarus without using his extra life.
entries.starts_with: Three Red Hearts and Book of Virtues.
entries.core_mechanic: Soul and Black Hearts become active-item charges instead of health, while Book of Virtues creates item-linked wisps.
weaknesses: Cannot use Soul or Black Hearts as health
Red Heart damage is especially costly

entries.name: Tainted Isaac
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Isaac.
entries.starts_with: Three Red Hearts and an eight-slot passive-item limit.
entries.core_mechanic: Most pedestals alternate between two choices, but only eight passive items can be held; Birthright raises the limit to twelve.
weaknesses: Bad timing can select the wrong pedestal option

entries.name: Tainted Azazel
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Azazel.
entries.starts_with: Three Black Hearts, flight, a long-range narrow Brimstone beam, and The Fool.
entries.core_mechanic: A short-range sneeze damages and marks enemies, causing the Brimstone beam to deal amplified damage.

entries.name: Tainted Apollyon
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Apollyon.
entries.starts_with: Two Red Hearts and Abyss in the pocket active slot.
entries.core_mechanic: Abyss converts unwanted pedestal items into permanent attacking locusts.
strengths: Locust army scales throughout the run
Abyss does not occupy the normal active slot
weaknesses: Many items create only a basic locust

tiers.label: Balanced or Specialist Picks
tiers.description: Capable characters whose mechanics are powerful in the right hands but less consistently forgiving.

entries.unlock: Defeat Mom's Heart ten times.
entries.starts_with: Three Soul Hearts and The Poop.
entries.core_mechanic: Cannot gain Red Heart containers and must rely on Soul, Black, Bone, or other non-red health.
strengths: Soul Heart damage preserves deal chance
weaknesses: Poor early damage can consume limited health

entries.name: Lazarus
entries.unlock: Have four or more Soul or Black Hearts at one time.
entries.starts_with: Three Red Hearts, a random pill, and one extra life.
entries.core_mechanic: Revives as Lazarus Risen with stronger offense; the extra life returns on later floors and planned deaths can grant permanent damage.
weaknesses: Poorly timed deaths can ruin health or deals

entries.name: Eden
entries.unlock: Defeat Mom's Heart or It Lives! to earn Eden Tokens; each Eden run consumes one token.
entries.starts_with: Random health, stats, one passive item, one active item, and random pickups.
entries.core_mechanic: Every run begins with a different build and stat distribution.

entries.name: Keeper
entries.unlock: Donate 1,000 coins to the Greed Donation Machine.
entries.starts_with: Two Coin Hearts and a triple shot; Wooden Nickel and a third Coin Heart are added through completion unlocks.
entries.core_mechanic: Uses coins as health and initially has a low health cap.
strengths: Coins provide health and purchasing power

entries.name: Apollyon
entries.unlock: Defeat Mega Satan.
entries.starts_with: Three Red Hearts and Void.
entries.core_mechanic: Void consumes passives for random stats and stores active-item effects to trigger together.
weaknesses: Void has a long charge time

entries.name: The Forgotten
entries.unlock: After defeating The Lamb, complete the Broken Shovel sequence and use Mom's Shovel on the grave in the Dark Room.
entries.starts_with: A Bone Heart body with a melee club and a Soul Heart spirit form with flight and spectral tears.
entries.core_mechanic: Switches between a powerful melee body and a mobile ranged soul tethered to the body.
weaknesses: The Soul is tethered to the body

tiers.label: High-Execution Characters
tiers.description: Strong characters whose health, inventory, reroll, or dual-control systems demand advanced planning.

entries.name: The Lost
entries.unlock: Die in a Sacrifice Room while holding Missing Poster.
entries.starts_with: Flight, spectral tears, no health, and Eternal D6 after its unlock; Holy Mantle is added after 879 Greed Donation Machine coins.
entries.core_mechanic: Dies from any unblocked hit. Holy Mantle blocks the first hit in each room, and Devil Deals can be taken without a health payment.
weaknesses: One-hit death after the shield breaks

entries.name: Jacob & Esau
entries.unlock: Defeat Mother.
entries.starts_with: Jacob has three Red Hearts; Esau has one Red Heart and one Soul Heart. Their stats and items are separate, but consumables are shared.
entries.core_mechanic: Both characters move together and can collect separate items from simultaneous double-pedestal choices.
strengths: Can obtain two items from selected choice rooms
weaknesses: Two hitboxes are difficult to dodge

entries.name: Tainted Cain
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Cain.
entries.starts_with: Two Red Hearts, one key, and Bag of Crafting in the pocket active slot.
entries.core_mechanic: Pedestal items break into pickups, and eight stored pickups are converted into a crafted item.

entries.name: Tainted Lazarus
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as Lazarus.
entries.starts_with: Alive Lazarus has Red Hearts, Dead Lazarus has Soul Hearts, and Flip occupies the pocket active slot.
entries.core_mechanic: Clearing rooms alternates between two forms with separate health, stats, and inventories. Flip changes forms and reveals paired faded items.
weaknesses: One weak form can block progression

tiers.label: Extreme Challenge
tiers.description: Characters designed around near-perfect play with little protection from a single mistake.

entries.name: Tainted Lost
entries.unlock: Use Red Key or Cracked Key on the hidden closet in Home while playing as The Lost.
entries.starts_with: Flight, spectral tears, no health, a Holy Card, and an innate damage multiplier.
entries.core_mechanic: Has no Holy Mantle. Many defensive or low-value items are filtered from the available pools, but Holy Card shields remain the main protection.
weaknesses: Any unshielded hit ends the run
Long bosses demand nearly flawless execution

isaacUnlockGuide.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Unlock Guide
isaacUnlockGuide.subtitle: Open the main endings first, obtain high-impact items and character upgrades, then complete alternate paths, Tainted characters, challenges, and final completion marks in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.
isaacUnlockGuide.intro: A useful progression route prioritizes unlocks that make future attempts stronger. Hard Mode is needed for selected rewards and final completion, while Greedier Mode provides a separate completion mark and important donation milestones.

phases.unlocks: The Womb chapter, Ending 1, and access to Mom's Heart on later runs.
phases.recommended_actions: Use Isaac or Azazel, visit each early Treasure Room, and enter Mom's fight with spare health.

phases.requirement: Defeat Mom's Heart ten times, then complete an eleventh Heart run.
phases.unlocks: The tenth defeat unlocks ???. The eleventh permanently replaces Mom's Heart with It Lives!.
phases.recommended_actions: Use several characters to begin earning completion rewards while learning Womb enemies and full-heart damage.
phases.next_target: Use the It Lives! exits to reach Cathedral and Sheol.

phases.requirement: After defeating It Lives!, enter the beam of light for Cathedral or the trapdoor for Sheol, then defeat Isaac or Satan.
phases.unlocks: Chapter V completion marks, character rewards, and progress toward the two Chapter VI routes.
phases.recommended_actions: Develop both routes while targeting useful character-specific unlocks.
phases.next_target: Defeat Isaac five times and Satan five times.

phases.requirement: Defeat Isaac five times to unlock The Polaroid and Satan five times to unlock The Negative.
phases.unlocks: The Polaroid opens Chest after Cathedral. The Negative opens Dark Room after Sheol.
phases.recommended_actions: Take the photograph that matches the intended route after defeating Mom.
phases.next_target: Defeat ??? in Chest and The Lamb in Dark Room.

phases.requirement: Clear Chest or Dark Room once, then bomb Angel statues in two Angel Rooms to assemble both Key Pieces and open the golden door in Chapter VI.
phases.unlocks: Mega Satan completion rewards; defeating Mega Satan unlocks Apollyon.
phases.recommended_actions: Skip the first Devil Room to force the next deal-room spawn into the Angel route, and preserve bombs for both statues.
phases.next_target: Pursue Delirium through available Void portals.

phases.requirement: Defeat Mom in under 20 minutes for Boss Rush and defeat Mom's Heart or It Lives! in under 30 minutes for the Blue Womb and Hush.
phases.unlocks: Character-specific completion marks, items, and progress toward the Repentance alternate path.
phases.recommended_actions: Attempt timed routes on strong runs, use map-reveal effects, and skip low-value rooms.

phases.requirement: Defeat Hush three times to unlock A Secret Exit.
phases.unlocks: Downpour or Dross, Mines or Ashpit, Mausoleum or Gehenna, the Knife Piece route, Corpse, and Mother.
phases.recommended_actions: The first alternate door costs one key, the second costs two bombs, and the Mausoleum or Gehenna entrance costs two full hearts. Collect both Knife Pieces and use the completed knife on the flesh door after Mom.
phases.next_target: Reach Corpse II and defeat Mother.

phases.requirement: Collect both Knife Pieces, enter Corpse, and defeat Mother.
phases.unlocks: Jacob and Esau, the Strange Door in Depths II, Challenge #39 Isaac's Awakening, and the route toward Home.
phases.recommended_actions: Prioritize health and defense because Mother has two long phases and boss armor.
phases.next_target: Complete the Ascent and defeat The Beast.

phases.phase: 9. Home, The Beast, and Tainted Characters
phases.requirement: In Depths II, defeat Mom, use The Fool to leave, open the Strange Door with The Polaroid or The Negative, reach Dad's Note, complete the Ascent, enter Home, and defeat The Beast route.
phases.unlocks: The final ending route and character-specific Beast rewards. Opening Mom's chest at Home grants Red Key on the first visit.
phases.recommended_actions: On later runs, leave a trinket in a Treasure or Boss Room before the Ascent. It becomes a Cracked Key in the corresponding Ascent room. Use Red Key or Cracked Key on Home's hidden closet to unlock the current Tainted character.
phases.next_target: Prioritize Tainted Isaac, Tainted Lilith, and other characters with strong rewards.

phases.requirement: Complete Greed Mode and donate coins after defeating Ultra Greed.
phases.unlocks: 30 coins unlock Wooden Nickel, 500 unlock Greedier Mode, 879 give The Lost a starting Holy Mantle, and 1,000 unlock Keeper.
phases.recommended_actions: Rotate characters to reduce the effect of the character-specific machine jam chance, and save coins for donation.
phases.next_target: Earn Greedier marks and Keeper's survival upgrades.

phases.unlocks: Defeat Isaac as ??? for Isaac's D6; Satan as Isaac for Mom's Knife; Isaac as Azazel for Satanic Bible; Isaac as Bethany for Book of Virtues; Isaac and ??? as Jacob and Esau for The Stairway and Birthright.
phases.recommended_actions: Obtain these rewards before spending many attempts on low-impact completion goals.

phases.requirement: Complete selected Beast and Delirium marks.
phases.unlocks: C Section from The Beast as Lilith; Twisted Pair from The Beast as Tainted Lilith; Glitched Crown from The Beast as Tainted Isaac; Spindown Dice from Delirium as Tainted Isaac; Sacred Orb from The Beast as Tainted Lost.
phases.recommended_actions: Begin with Lilith, Tainted Lilith, and Tainted Isaac. Attempt Sacred Orb after becoming comfortable with Holy Card management and final bosses.
phases.next_target: Target Rock Bottom, Godhead, Mega Mush, and Death Certificate.

phases.requirement: Complete selected numbered challenges and general achievements.
phases.unlocks: High Brow unlocks Jera; Demo Man unlocks Chaos Card; Cursed! unlocks Credit Card; Solar System unlocks Perthro; Isaac's Awakening unlocks Spirit Sword; five consecutive Daily Challenge wins unlock Cracked Crown.
phases.recommended_actions: Unlock at least six runes or Soul Stones so Rune Shards stop appearing, then prioritize reroll, duplication, combat, and free-purchase rewards.
phases.next_target: Complete the remaining character post-it notes.

phases.requirement: Finish the required Hard Mode and Greedier marks, unlock every ending and secret, complete all other achievements, and pick up every item at least once.
phases.unlocks: All non-Tainted Hard Mode post-it notes unlock Mega Mush. All normal and Tainted post-it notes unlock Death Certificate. Completing the entire collection leads to Dead God.
phases.recommended_actions: Finish powerful characters first to improve the item pool, then complete difficult characters such as Jacob and Esau, The Lost, and Tainted Lost on a mature save file.
phases.next_target: Dead God on the current save file.

isaacBossesAndEndings.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Bosses and Endings
isaacBossesAndEndings.subtitle: Follow every major route from Mom's Heart to The Beast. Each entry explains access requirements, signature attacks, preparation priorities, and the completion mark or ending earned in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.
isaacBossesAndEndings.intro: Repentance opens its endgame in layers. Early Mom's Heart victories unlock It Lives, Cathedral, Sheol, Blue Womb, and later alternate floors; deeper routes require the Polaroid, the Negative, Key Pieces, Knife Pieces, or the Strange Door. Use the route notes below to plan each run before committing at Mom, It Lives, or the alternate-path doors.

bosses.heading: Mom's Heart and It Lives
bosses.route: Defeat Mom to open Womb or Utero, then clear the second floor of Chapter IV. Mom's Heart is the final boss until It Lives permanently replaces it after 11 Mom's Heart victories.
requirements: Defeat Mom once to unlock Chapter IV.
Defeat Mom's Heart 10 times to unlock the Blue Womb route.
Defeat Mom's Heart 11 times to unlock It Lives, which permanently replaces Mom's Heart.
attacks: Alternates between hiding in the ceiling and returning to the center while summoning enemies.
Fires rings, spirals, and aimed spreads of red tears.
preparation: Enter Chapter IV with enough health for full-heart damage.
Prioritize sustained damage, movement speed, and a way to clear summoned enemies quickly.
bosses.result: Awards the heart completion mark. The early Mom's Heart victories advance Endings 2-11 and unlock major progression features.

bosses.heading: Isaac
bosses.route: After defeating It Lives, enter the beam of light to reach Cathedral. Defeat Isaac there. After unlocking the Polaroid by defeating Isaac five times, carry it from Mom and enter Isaac's End Chest to continue to the Chest.
requirements: Unlock It Lives by defeating Mom's Heart 11 times.
Defeat Isaac five times to unlock the Polaroid for future Mom fights.
attacks: Fires cardinal and diagonal tear spreads while repositioning around the room.
Later phases add dense holy tear patterns and Crack the Sky-style beams marked by light on the floor.
preparation: Keep moving between safe lanes instead of circling close to the boss.
Projectile blocking, orbitals, and high movement speed make the beam phase safer.
bosses.result: Awards the cross completion mark and Ending 13. The Polaroid route continues through the Chest to ???.

bosses.heading: Satan
bosses.route: After defeating It Lives, enter the trapdoor to reach Sheol. The fight begins with The Fallen, continues against Satan's upper body, and ends with a stomping leg phase. After unlocking the Negative by defeating Satan five times, carry it from Mom and enter Satan's End Chest to reach the Dark Room.
requirements: Unlock It Lives by defeating Mom's Heart 11 times.
Defeat Satan five times to unlock the Negative for future Mom fights.
attacks: The Fallen opens the fight with charges, split shots, and Brimstone attacks.
Satan fires aimed tears and horizontal or vertical Brimstone lasers.
preparation: Save burst damage for The Fallen so the main phases begin cleanly.
Watch Satan's face and body orientation for laser tells.
bosses.result: Awards the inverted-cross completion mark and Ending 12. The Negative route continues through the Dark Room to The Lamb.

bosses.heading: Mega Satan
bosses.route: Complete Chapter VI, then defeat Angels to collect Key Piece 1 and Key Piece 2. Carry both pieces to the Chest or Dark Room and open the large golden door in the starting room.
requirements: Complete Chapter VI by defeating ??? or The Lamb to enable Key Pieces.
Bomb Angel statues or otherwise fight Angels and collect both Key Pieces.
Reach the Chest with the Polaroid or the Dark Room with the Negative.
attacks: Alternates direct hand, laser, projectile, and rock-wave attacks with enemy waves.
Summons Deadly Sins, Horsemen, Angels, and other bosses during stagger phases.
preparation: Bring strong room-clear damage for summoned waves and consistent single-target damage for Mega Satan.
Projectile protection, invulnerability, and high health are especially valuable.
bosses.result: Awards the brimstone completion mark and Ending 16. On victory, the run may open a Void gateway; otherwise it ends immediately.

bosses.heading: Hush
bosses.route: After defeating Mom's Heart 10 times, defeat Mom's Heart or It Lives before 30:00. Enter the large Blue Womb door, use the preparation rooms, and fight Hush.
requirements: Defeat Mom's Heart 10 times to unlock Blue Womb.
Defeat Mom's Heart or It Lives before the 30-minute in-game timer.
Remain in the boss room before the timed door disappears.
attacks: Begins with a Blue Baby-like first form before revealing the main body.
Uses dense rings, continuum tears that wrap across the screen, Hush Flies, burrowing, and sky lasers.
preparation: Prioritize movement speed, health, sustained damage, and projectile defense.
Use the Blue Womb's two treasure rooms and shop before entering the fight.
bosses.result: Awards the Hush completion mark and Ending 17. Defeating Hush always opens a gateway to the Void and defeating Hush three times unlocks the alternate-floor path.

bosses.heading: Delirium
bosses.route: Enter a Void gateway after a major boss. Hush always opens one; It Lives, Isaac, Satan, ???, The Lamb, and Mega Satan can also open one after the Void has been unlocked. Explore the Void until the boss room containing Delirium is found.
requirements: Defeat Hush once to unlock the Void.
Use a Void gateway after an eligible endgame boss.
attacks: Rapidly transforms into bosses encountered during the run and copies their attacks.
Teleports frequently and can chain forms with very different movement speeds and hitboxes.
preparation: Maximize survivability before entering the Void because the correct boss room is not identified in advance.
High speed, invulnerability effects, projectile blocking, and consistent homing are strong.
bosses.result: Awards the torn-paper completion mark and Ending 20.

bosses.heading: Mother
bosses.route: After defeating Hush three times, enter Downpour or Dross with one key, Mines or Ashpit with two bombs, and Mausoleum or Gehenna by paying two full hearts. Collect Knife Piece 1 in the Downpour II mirror world and Knife Piece 2 in the Mines II abandoned mineshaft. Use the completed knife on the flesh door after the alternate Mom fight, defeat the alternate Mom's Heart, then continue through Corpse I and Corpse II.
requirements: Defeat Hush three times to unlock A Secret Exit and the alternate floors.
In Downpour or Dross II, touch the white fire, enter the mirror, and take Knife Piece 1 from the mirrored treasure room.
In Mines or Ashpit II, press all three yellow buttons, enter the abandoned mineshaft, and escape with Knife Piece 2.
attacks: Phase one uses a thrown knife, a bouncing ball-and-fistula attack, maggots, and layered tear spreads.
Phase two adds sweeping hand attacks, fast charges, bouncing projectiles, and dense wall-to-wall tear lines.
preparation: Enter Corpse with strong movement speed, high health, and enough damage for two long phases.
Save shields, invulnerability, and burst effects for phase two.
bosses.result: Awards the knife completion mark and Ending 21. The first Mother victory unlocks the Strange Door route to Home.

bosses.heading: Dogma
bosses.route: After defeating Mother once, bomb the marked skull in Depths II for The Fool. Defeat Mom, take the Polaroid or Negative, teleport out, and use it to open the Strange Door. Reach the special Mausoleum II, take Dad's Note, complete the Ascension to Home, sleep in Mom's bed, and approach the television in the living room.
requirements: Defeat Mother once to unlock the Strange Door.
Bring the Polaroid or Negative back to the starting room of Depths II.
Take Dad's Note and finish the reverse-floor Ascension.
attacks: The television form emits static projectiles and large beam attacks.
The second form charges, dives, circles the room, and releases rotating feather or halo patterns.
preparation: Use Home's bed before the fight to restore health.
Keep open space around Isaac for Dogma's charge and spinning patterns.
bosses.result: Dogma is the first stage of the Beast route. Defeating it grants Dogma's defensive effect and advances directly to the final gauntlet.

bosses.heading: The Beast
bosses.route: After Dogma, defeat Ultra Famine, Ultra Pestilence, Ultra War, and Ultra Death in sequence, then fight The Beast in a horizontal scrolling arena.
requirements: Complete the Strange Door, Dad's Note, Ascension, Home, and Dogma sequence.
attacks: Each Ultra Horseman has a separate aerial fight with projectile spreads and charge patterns.
The Beast uses fire rings, lunges, roars, stalactites, and long chase sequences through flames.
preparation: Build for a long multi-boss sequence rather than one short burst fight.
Health, damage reduction, orbitals, homing, and sustained damage are stronger than room-clear effects.
bosses.result: Awards the note completion mark and Ending 22, the final main-story ending in Repentance.

bosses.heading: Alternate Floors, Endings, and Completion Marks
bosses.route: The main branches are Cathedral to the Chest, Sheol to the Dark Room, Blue Womb to Hush, the Void to Delirium, the Knife Piece route to Corpse and Mother, and the Strange Door route to Home, Dogma, and The Beast.
requirements: Polaroid: defeat Isaac five times; carry it through Cathedral to enter the Chest.
Negative: defeat Satan five times; carry it through Sheol to enter the Dark Room.
Key Pieces: defeat Angels after completing Chapter VI; use both to open Mega Satan's door.
Alternate floors: defeat Hush three times; pay one key, two bombs, and two full hearts at the three route doors.
attacks: Completion marks track Mom's Heart or It Lives, Isaac, Satan, Boss Rush, ???, The Lamb, Mega Satan, Hush, Delirium, Mother, The Beast, and Greed or Greedier.
Hard Mode victories add blood to the corresponding marks; Greedier upgrades the Greed mark.
preparation: Choose the intended branch before Mom so the correct Polaroid, Negative, timed door, or alternate path is available.
Use Hard Mode when collecting marks to earn the normal and Hard versions together.
bosses.result: A completed post-it note records the character's major route victories and unlocks character-specific rewards.

isaacChallenges.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Challenges and Rewards
isaacChallenges.subtitle: Compare every challenge by starting setup, special rules, destination, difficulty, and permanent reward in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance. Reward priority highlights the clears that improve future runs the most.
isaacChallenges.intro: Repentance contains 45 preset challenge runs. Challenge runs block normal achievement progress, but completing the stated destination unlocks a specific rune, card, item, trinket, pickup type, pill effect, or character upgrade. The highest-impact early targets are High Brow for Jera, Solar System for Perthro, Waka Waka for Death's Touch, The Family Man for Epic Fetus, and Isaac's Awakening for Spirit Sword.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: Permanent Curse of Darkness and Curse of the Unknown; shops cannot spawn; treasure rooms remain available.
challenges.strategy: Treat it like a normal Isaac run and use the visible map layout to compensate for darkness.

challenges.setup: Number One, E. Coli, Butt Bombs, Flush!, and Petrified Poop.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Prioritize range because Number One sharply reduces it.

challenges.setup: Tiny Planet, Soy Milk, The Small Rock, and Iron Bar.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Fight at medium range and let the orbiting tear ring pass through enemies.

challenges.setup: Sacrificial Dagger, Dark Matter, Dead Bird, Pentagram, Razor Blade, and Whore of Babylon.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Use Sacrificial Dagger aggressively on early floors and keep red health low enough to maintain Whore of Babylon.

challenges.setup: Infamy, Thunder Thighs, Bucket of Lard, and Yum Heart.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; starting speed is extremely low.
challenges.strategy: Speed upgrades are the first priority.

challenges.setup: Distant Admiration, Forever Alone, Halo of Flies, and Transcendence.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; Isaac cannot fire tears; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Use flight to hide over gaps and rocks while orbitals deal contact damage.

challenges.setup: Ipecac, Mr. Mega, My Reflection, and one random pill.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; reflected Ipecac shots can easily damage Lazarus.
challenges.strategy: Fire from open lanes and wait for the returning arc before moving forward.

challenges.setup: Extra health, Guppy's Head, Guppy's Hairball, and Guppy's Tail.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn; the Hairball and blue flies are the main starting damage.
challenges.strategy: Swing the Hairball through enemies, then retreat.

challenges.setup: Remote Detonator, Dr. Fetus, and Match Stick.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Use Dr. Fetus from long range and place bombs where enemies will remain until detonation.

challenges.setup: Blue Map, Treasure Map, The Compass, Raw Liver, Yum Heart, and Child's Heart.
challenges.rules: Every normal doorway is lined with damaging spikes; special-room and secret-room entrances are not.
challenges.strategy: Plan the shortest route with the mapping items and avoid backtracking.

challenges.setup: Epic Fetus and Loki's Horns.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Focus the intended Epic Fetus crosshair and ignore the extra Loki's Horns targets.

challenges.setup: 9 Volt, Habit, Lemon Mishap, and one Lemon Party pill.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Place Lemon Mishap where enemies must cross it, especially near doors and in narrow rooms.

challenges.setup: 9 Volt, The Bean, Black Bean, Butt Bombs, and Pyro.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Conserve Butt Bombs and use The Bean whenever enemies group near Isaac.

challenges.setup: 9 Volt, The Battery, Deck of Cards, Starter Deck, and two random tarot cards.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Cycle Deck of Cards often and save high-impact cards for bosses or dangerous rooms.

challenges.setup: Polyphemus, Cupid's Arrow, and My Reflection.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; damage is fixed at 100 and fire rate at 0.50.
challenges.strategy: Ignore damage and tears upgrades; seek range, homing, multishot, and other tear modifiers.

challenges.setup: Spoon Bender, Technology, and Technology 2.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: The homing lasers are already strong enough to clear quickly.

challenges.setup: Anti-Gravity and Strange Attractor.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Tap fire to release tears quickly instead of building unstable clusters.

challenges.setup: The Mulligan, Spiderbaby, and Tick.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; Tick cannot normally be dropped.
challenges.strategy: Lean on fly generation and seek damage or luck.

challenges.setup: BFFS!, Rotten Baby, Brother Bobby, Sister Maggy, and Dad's Key.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Collect familiars, orbitals, Box of Friends, and familiar-scaling effects.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Treat shops, libraries, boss items, and Angel or Devil Rooms as the main build sources.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: Every floor is much larger than normal.
challenges.reward: Gold Hearts can appear in future runs
challenges.strategy: Buy mapping and speed upgrades, then clear only what the build needs.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: Most gameplay is accelerated; after 16 minutes Isaac takes damage every 10 seconds.
challenges.reward: Get Out of Jail Free card
challenges.strategy: Skip low-value rooms, take fast damage upgrades, and avoid lengthy reroll setups.

challenges.setup: Kamikaze!, Pyromaniac, Mr. Mega, and Brother Bobby.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.reward: Gold Bombs can appear in future runs
challenges.strategy: Kamikaze! heals instead of hurting because of Pyromaniac, giving repeatable damage and recovery.

challenges.setup: Money = Power, Sack of Pennies, and 50 coins.
challenges.rules: Every normal door costs one coin; Isaac drops coins when hit; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Keep a coin reserve for mandatory doors and avoid unnecessary branches.

challenges.setup: Twelve red-heart containers with only one heart filled, plus Charm of the Vampire.
challenges.rules: Red-heart pickups become coins and many regeneration items do not work.
challenges.strategy: Use Charm of the Vampire, health-restoring cards, beds, and non-pickup healing.

challenges.setup: Key Piece 1, Key Piece 2, The Ladder, The Boomerang, Trinity Shield, and Mom's Knife.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Mom's Knife supplies enough damage for the full route.

challenges.setup: Thunder Thighs and three Bob's Brain familiars.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn; Bob's Brain explosions can damage the player.
challenges.strategy: Fire only with open space between Isaac and the target.

challenges.setup: Rainbow Worm, Rainbow Baby, and 3 Dollar Bill.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; Rainbow Worm cannot be dropped.
challenges.strategy: Pause before entering dangerous rooms when Rainbow Worm is showing an awkward pattern.

challenges.setup: Book of Belial and Chocolate Milk.
challenges.rules: Every tear that misses an enemy damages Judas.
challenges.strategy: Use fully charged, deliberate shots at large targets.

challenges.setup: Holy Grail, Punching Bag, Isaac's Heart, and Spear of Destiny.
challenges.rules: Isaac's Heart follows Punching Bag and is the vulnerable hitbox; Isaac cannot rely on normal tears.
challenges.strategy: Body-block threats with invulnerable Isaac while keeping the wandering heart away from enemies.

challenges.setup: Random high health, 10 random items, and both Key Pieces.
challenges.rules: Starts at Mega Satan and moves backward to Basement I; one base item and one red-heart container are lost on each ascent.
challenges.strategy: Restart weak item sets before attempting Mega Satan.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: HUD, pickups, item sprites, prices, and map information are misleading; every boss is The Bloat.
challenges.reward: Magdalene starts with a Full Health pill
challenges.strategy: Judge pickups by their effect after collection instead of appearance.

challenges.setup: Friendly Ball and Mom's Eyeshadow.
challenges.rules: Friendly Ball creates a charmed monster and recharges when that monster dies; treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.reward: Charged Keys can appear in future runs
challenges.strategy: Capture durable or high-damage enemies and replace weak allies often.

challenges.setup: Both Key Pieces.
challenges.rules: Permanent curses, no heart pickups, mostly champion enemies, and double bosses whenever possible.
challenges.strategy: Restart until the first two treasure rooms provide major damage or renewable healing.

challenges.setup: Cupid's Arrow and Rubber Cement.
challenges.rules: Damage is fixed at 100 and fire rate at 0.20.
challenges.strategy: Line up piercing ricochets to hit several enemies with each slow shot.

challenges.setup: 9 Volt, The Poop, Dirty Mind, three Butt Bombs, Skatole, E. Coli, BFFS!, Thunder Thighs, and Mysterious Candy.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn.
challenges.strategy: Break every poop to create friendly dips and use BFFS! to amplify them.

challenges.setup: Blood Oath, Blood Bag, Anemic, Book of Belial, and Child's Heart.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; Blood Oath removes red health at the start of each floor for temporary stats.
challenges.strategy: Seek renewable healing, Holy Mantle-style protection, and health generation.

challenges.setup: Urn of Souls, Schoolbag, Guppy's Paw, and Maggy's Faith.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn; Urn of Souls consumes collected souls as fuel.
challenges.strategy: Group enemies before firing the Urn and conserve charges for bosses.

challenges.setup: Spirit Sword, Trinity Shield, and Mom's Bracelet.
challenges.rules: Treasure rooms cannot spawn; the route ends at Mother.
challenges.strategy: Use charged sword spins to erase projectiles and Trinity Shield to cover the facing direction.

challenges.setup: 20/20 on both characters; Esau also has X-Ray Vision.
challenges.rules: Every enemy and boss spawns twice.
challenges.strategy: Keep Jacob and Esau tightly aligned and prioritize room-wide damage, piercing, and defensive items.

challenges.setup: Mom's Box, Mom's Purse, and Marbles.
challenges.rules: All pedestal items become trinkets; Marbles activates on every hit.
challenges.strategy: Use Mom's Box constantly and swallow the strongest trinkets before replacing them.

challenges.setup: No starting items.
challenges.rules: Blindfolded; treasure rooms cannot spawn; the body automatically explodes every 2.5 seconds and is the main weapon.
challenges.strategy: Throw the body, move the soul to safety, and time explosions before retrieving it.

challenges.setup: Bag of Crafting.
challenges.rules: Cards and pills become item cards; using one grants a random item effect for the current room; boss rewards expand the hand and provide more item cards.
challenges.strategy: Save powerful item cards for bosses and use weaker effects to clear normal rooms.

challenges.setup: Dad's Key and Red Key.
challenges.rules: Floors begin isolated; use infinite Red Key charges to reveal rooms. Dark Esau appears after 20 uses, with another added every five additional uses.
challenges.strategy: Use mapping items to locate likely special-room and boss positions with fewer Red Key openings.

challenges.setup: TMTRAINER.
challenges.rules: Glitched items have randomized effects; floor visuals and shop prices are randomized.
challenges.strategy: Test glitched effects in safe rooms and avoid repeatedly triggering effects that damage, teleport, or soft-lock the run.

isaacBestMods.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Best Mods
isaacBestMods.subtitle: Improve item information, navigation, boss readability, music, practice, and endgame variety for The Binding of Isaac: Repentance. Every card includes branch compatibility, dependencies, and the main conflict to avoid.
isaacBestMods.intro: For a stable loadout, begin with one information overlay, one boss-bar system, and a small number of quality-of-life mods. Add music frameworks only when a soundtrack requires them, and test large content expansions on their own before combining them. Repentance+ also includes newer built-in interface improvements, while Workshop tools such as External Item Descriptions remain useful for deeper statistics and modded content.

mods.name: External Item Descriptions
mods.best_for: Learning items without leaving the game
mods.description: Shows detailed descriptions for collectibles, trinkets, cards, runes, pills, horse pills, and selected rooms. It also supports Spindown Dice results, Bag of Crafting information, transformations, and a large number of content mods.
mods.requirements: No mandatory framework; Mod Config Menu is recommended for in-game settings.
mods.conflicts: Avoid displaying it beside another full item-description overlay unless both have been positioned separately.

mods.name: GoodTrip [Fixed]
mods.description: Lets players teleport between cleared rooms directly from the minimap. Mouse, keyboard, and controller navigation are supported, while combat rooms and uncleared destinations remain blocked.
mods.requirements: The fixed edition is standalone; the original GoodTrip mod is not required.
mods.conflicts: Use only the fixed edition, and review minimap-control bindings when combining it with other map mods.

mods.name: Enhanced Boss Bars
mods.description: Replaces the standard boss display with individual icons, responsive health bars, and more than 45 special boss-bar designs. It is especially useful for segmented bosses and rooms containing several bosses.
mods.requirements: Mod Config Menu is optional for visual customization.
mods.conflicts: Disable every other mod that changes boss health bars; overlapping boss-bar mods can produce duplicate vanilla and custom bars.

mods.name: Planetarium Chance [REP+]
mods.description: Adds the current floor's Planetarium chance to the HUD and accounts for relevant collectibles and modifiers. It makes Planetarium routing readable without manual percentage tracking.
mods.requirements: The Repentance DLC and the current Repentance+ branch.
mods.conflicts: Reposition HUD elements when another stat or chance display occupies the same screen area.

mods.name: Practice Mode
mods.description: Adds a practice challenge for Mega Satan, Hush, Delirium, Mother, and Dogma with the final gauntlet. The mode supplies a realistic minimum build, provides unlimited health, and tracks how many times the player is hit.
mods.conflicts: Use a separate practice profile and disable gameplay-altering mods when learning vanilla attack patterns.

mods.name: Da Rules
mods.best_for: Custom experiments, spawning, and rule-based practice
mods.description: Adds a sandbox menu for replacing, resizing, moving, spawning, and modifying enemies, pickups, visuals, and room behavior. It is useful for testing synergies, recreating situations, and building custom practice rules.
mods.requirements: Open the full menu from the starting room of a floor; the default configurable menu key is J.
mods.conflicts: Complex rule sets can interfere with content mods that rewrite the same entities or room events.

mods.name: Antibirth Music+++
mods.description: Replaces the default soundtrack with an expanded and polished Antibirth music set, including alternate shop and arcade tracks and additional boss music options.
mods.requirements: Music Mod Callback is required for several advanced track behaviors; Mod Config Menu enables its options.
mods.conflicts: When used with Soundtrack Menu, enable the mod's Passive Mode. Do not combine multiple mods that replace the same music configuration without a soundtrack manager.

mods.name: Soundtrack Menu
mods.description: Adds an in-game menu for selecting music packs and assigning tracks. It allows several compatible soundtrack mods to coexist without permanently replacing one another.
mods.requirements: Use REPENTOGON or the correct Repentance/Repentance+ Music Mod Callback, but not both at the same time.
mods.conflicts: Installing both the Afterbirth+ and Repentance versions of Music Mod Callback can break playback.

mods.name: Music Mod Callback
mods.description: Provides music callbacks used by soundtrack replacements and music-selection tools. It does nothing by itself and should only be installed when another subscribed mod lists it as a requirement.
mods.conflicts: Disable the separate Afterbirth+ edition and do not run it together with REPENTOGON when the soundtrack tool instructs users to choose only one framework.

mods.name: Fiend Folio: Reheated
mods.description: Adds more than 500 monsters, 23 bosses, more than 140 items, over 48,000 rooms, two major playable character concepts, and 10 challenges. It significantly changes floor variety and difficulty.
mods.requirements: Custom Stage API must be subscribed and enabled.
mods.conflicts: Large room, enemy, stage, and content packs can overlap; test this mod with a small loadout before adding more overhauls.

mods.name: Installation and Activation
mods.description: Open a Workshop page, select Subscribe, let Steam finish the download, launch the game, open Mods from the main menu, and enable the mod plus every listed dependency. Restart the game after installing frameworks, large content packs, or music systems.
mods.compatibility: Match the Workshop title and description to the active game branch: Repentance or Repentance+.
mods.requirements: Steam Workshop access and the DLC listed on the mod page.
mods.conflicts: Do not subscribe to obsolete and maintained replacements at the same time.

mods.name: Load Order and Troubleshooting
mods.best_for: Fixing crashes, missing menus, duplicate HUDs, and broken audio
mods.description: Isaac does not include a drag-and-drop load-order manager. Keep required frameworks enabled, remove obsolete duplicates, and add mods in small batches. If a problem appears, disable half the loadout, restart, and repeat until the conflict is isolated.
mods.compatibility: Keep all enabled mods on the same Repentance or Repentance+ branch and use a mod-free setup for Daily Runs and online matchmaking.
mods.requirements: Back up save data before testing large overhauls and check each Workshop page's required-items panel.
mods.conflicts: The most common conflicts are duplicate item descriptions, two boss-bar replacements, multiple music systems, old dependency versions, and several stage overhauls editing the same floors.

isaacOnlineCoop.title: The Binding of Isaac Repentance Plus Online Co-op
isaacOnlineCoop.subtitle: Set up a two-to-four-player Steam run, choose the right matchmaking option, and understand how characters, resources, unlocks, communication, and official updates work online in Repentance+.
isaacOnlineCoop.intro: Repentance+ is the free Steam DLC that adds integrated online co-op to Repentance. Up to four full characters can enter the same run through Friend Match, Public Match, or Quick Match, with challenges, Greed Mode, Daily Runs, achievements, completion marks, chat, emotes, and multiplayer-focused balance changes included.

steps.heading: Install the Required Steam Content
details: Every player needs Rebirth, Afterbirth, Afterbirth+, Repentance, and the free Repentance+ DLC on Steam.
Use the same current Repentance+ version and restart after updates.

options.use: Create a lobby for up to four players from the host's Steam friends list.
Host or join a visible lobby with other online players.

steps.heading: Select Characters
details: Each player selects a full character available on that player's Repentance+ save.
Health, passive and active items, cards, pills, and trinkets are individual; coins, bombs, and keys are shared.
Combine durable, damage, and utility characters so the group does not compete for every upgrade.

steps.heading: Choose a Supported Mode
details: Online play supports Normal, Hard, Greed, Greedier, Challenges, and Daily Runs.
Choose Hard or Greedier when the intended completion mark requires the harder setting.

steps.heading: Share Items Fairly
details: Many pedestals still go to one player, so assign build-defining items by synergy and rotate general upgrades.
Use the emote wheel's dice roll when several players want the same item.
Spend shared coins, bombs, and keys only after the group agrees.

steps.heading: Earn Progress Together
details: Eligible players can earn achievements, unlocks, and character completion marks in online runs.
Normal route, difficulty, challenge, and prerequisite requirements still apply to each player's save.
Plan one destination that clears missing marks for several selected characters.

steps.heading: Communicate and Moderate
details: Use the emote wheel to mark routes, tinted rocks, secret rooms, pickups, and item choices.
Use group text chat for route and resource decisions.
Use vote kick when a public player repeatedly disrupts the match.

steps.heading: Fix Connection or Compatibility Problems
details: Check DLC ownership and version matching first.
Disable Workshop mods, restart the game, and verify files through Steam.
Recreate the lobby with another host when joining or synchronization still fails.

More online bug fixes and improvements

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Binding of Isaac: Repentance

questions.question: What is The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: The Binding of Isaac: Repentance is the final major DLC for the roguelike dungeon shooter The Binding of Isaac. It adds hundreds of items, 17 Tainted characters, alternate floors, new superbosses including Mother, Dogma, and The Beast, and the Repentance+ online co-op mode.

questions.question: How many items are in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: The Binding of Isaac: Repentance contains over 700 collectible items plus trinkets, cards, runes, and pills. Many unlock through character completion marks, challenges, and donation milestones, so a strong progression route improves the item pool over time.

questions.question: Do I need Repentance+ for online co-op in The Binding of Isaac?
questions.answer: Yes. Repentance+ is the free Steam DLC that adds integrated online co-op to The Binding of Isaac: Repentance. Every player needs the full DLC set and the same current Repentance+ version to join Friend Match, Public Match, or Quick Match.

questions.question: What are Tainted characters in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: Tainted characters are alternate, harder-to-play versions of each normal character in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance. Each is unlocked from the hidden closet in Home using Red Key or Cracked Key after reaching the Strange Door route through Mother and The Beast.

questions.question: How do I unlock the alternate floors in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: In The Binding of Isaac: Repentance, defeat Hush three times to unlock A Secret Exit, then pay one key, two bombs, and two full hearts at the three route doors. This opens Downpour or Dross, Mines or Ashpit, and Mausoleum or Gehenna, leading to Corpse and the Mother boss fight.

questions.question: Where can I play The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: The Binding of Isaac: Repentance is available on Steam for Windows, with Linux and Steam Deck support through Proton. Console versions exist on Nintendo Switch, PlayStation, and Xbox, though online co-op and mod support are PC-focused.

questions.question: Does The Binding of Isaac: Repentance support mods?
questions.answer: Yes. The Binding of Isaac: Repentance supports Steam Workshop mods for interface overlays, boss bars, practice tools, music packs, and large content expansions. Repentance+ also adds newer built-in interface improvements.

questions.question: What is Dead God completion in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance?
questions.answer: Dead God is the final completion milestone in The Binding of Isaac: Repentance. It is reached by finishing every Hard Mode and Greedier completion mark, unlocking every ending and secret, completing all achievements, and picking up every item at least once.
